title: Lagenophora billardierei
intro: Lagenophora billardierei, also known as Billygoat's beard, is a perennial herb native to eastern Australia, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used by indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, and modern research has confirmed its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in wound healing, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Lagenophora billardierei include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lagenophora billardierei is often made into teas, infusions, and topical preparations, such as creams and ointments, to leverage its healing potential.
taxonomy: Lagenophora billardierei is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is native to Australia and is part of the genus Lagenophora. The classification of Lagenophora billardierei is as follows: it is a member of the family Asteraceae, subfamily Asteroideae, tribe Gnaphalieae, and genus Lagenophora. Lagenophora billardierei is also known as Hill's cudweed or billardiere's cudweed. This species is closely related to other species within the same genus.
